title: Guidance Specification
description: Analyze topic and generate guidance-specification.md: extract 5-10 core terms, define non-goals, decompose into features (max 8), apply RFC 2119 keywords. Output: guidance-specification.md with sections for positioning, terminology, non-goals, feature decomposition.
topic: RevDeck: brainstorm more versatile and specialized reverse-engineering features, plus plugin specification and SDK for extension
findings: Generated guidance spec for RevDeck plugin SDK and specialized reverse-engineering expansion: 10 terms, responsible-use boundaries, 8 feature candidates, RFC 2119 cross-cutting requirements, assumptions/open questions. Appended 10 valid JSONL discoveries.

title: Product Manager Analysis
description: Analyze from product-manager perspective: product strategy, prioritization, roadmap, user value, packaging, plugin marketplace viability, and MVP tradeoffs. Reference guidance-specification.md.
topic: RevDeck: brainstorm more versatile and specialized reverse-engineering features, plus plugin specification and SDK for extension
findings: PM view: prioritize local SDK preview over marketplace. P0 sequence is manifest, stable schema, adapter SDK, permissions, and minimal harness. Adapter-first value beats broad custom lenses; plugin findings need suggestion/draft/confirmed trust states.

title: System Architect Analysis
description: Analyze from system-architect perspective: technical architecture, plugin runtime, ABI/API boundaries, job model, safety, scalability, integration patterns. MUST include Data Model, State Machine, Error Handling, Observability, Configuration Model. Reference guidance-specification.md.
topic: RevDeck: brainstorm more versatile and specialized reverse-engineering features, plus plugin specification and SDK for extension
findings: Architecture recommends process-based, schema-validated plugins with host-mediated graph commits, first-class permissions, deterministic run digests, plugin run state machine, metrics, and layered config. P0 scope: manifest, stable SDK schemas, adapter contracts, execution safety.

title: Data Architect Analysis
description: Analyze from data-architect perspective: unified object graph, schema evolution, plugin-owned tables, migrations, artifact storage, query model, provenance, import/export formats. Reference guidance-specification.md.
topic: RevDeck: brainstorm more versatile and specialized reverse-engineering features, plus plugin specification and SDK for extension
findings: Proposes host-owned core object graph plus schema-registered typed attributes/datasets; ObjectBatch write API; contribution/provenance records; content-addressed artifact storage; stable query APIs without SQL; staged plugin extensibility.

title: Subject Matter Expert Analysis
description: Analyze from reverse-engineering subject-matter-expert perspective: domain-specific labs, workflows, analyzers, edge cases for binaries/firmware/traces/crashes/protocol/memory, responsible-use boundaries. Reference guidance-specification.md.
topic: RevDeck: brainstorm more versatile and specialized reverse-engineering features, plus plugin specification and SDK for extension
findings: SME analysis recommends adapter-first SDK, core RE evidence object families, explicit address-space normalization, confidence on inferred domain facts, cross-lab evidence chains, and default redaction/least-privilege responsible-use boundaries.

title: UI Designer Analysis
description: Analyze from ui-designer perspective: TUI information architecture, lens layout, command deck, plugin UI slots, cockpit aesthetics, keyboard model, discoverability, responsive terminal breakpoints. Reference guidance-specification.md.
topic: RevDeck: brainstorm more versatile and specialized reverse-engineering features, plus plugin specification and SDK for extension
findings: UI design recommends host-owned plugin slots, declarative lens registry, Command Deck as discovery layer, visible provenance/permissions, constrained templates before custom renderers, and terminal breakpoint validation.

title: Test Strategist Analysis
description: Analyze from test-strategist perspective: plugin SDK contract tests, golden fixtures, sandbox tests, integration matrix, compatibility, fuzzing, CLI/TUI regression strategy. Reference guidance-specification.md.
topic: RevDeck: brainstorm more versatile and specialized reverse-engineering features, plus plugin specification and SDK for extension
findings: Test strategy recommends SDK conformance suite, golden graph fixtures, deterministic replay, sandbox permission matrix, compatibility gates, fuzz targets, and reducer-first CLI/TUI regressions before marketplace or broad custom plugin APIs.

title: Synthesis + Feature Index
description: Cross-role synthesis: analyze all role outputs for consensus, conflicts, unique contributions. Generate feature specs F-001 through F-00N, feature-index.json, and synthesis-changelog.md. Resolve conflicts with [RESOLVED]/[SUGGESTED]/[UNRESOLVED] tags.
topic: RevDeck: brainstorm more versatile and specialized reverse-engineering features, plus plugin specification and SDK for extension
findings: Synthesis completed manually after interrupted wave: local SDK preview, process-based schema protocol, host-mediated ObjectBatch commits, adapter-first proof plugins, least-privilege permissions, declarative lens slots, conformance suite, and analyst-gated finding suggestions.
